skill.name: normalize
skill.description: Normalize design to match your design system and ensure consistency
skill.summary: Automatically aligns UI components with your design system standards for consistent, maintainable interfaces
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 33 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns, all false positives from instructional emphasis markers like 'CRITICAL', 'IMPORTANT', 'NEVER' in documentation. The skill performs read-only design system analysis with no code execution, network requests, or system commands. All findings dismissed as non-security instructional text.

content.user_title: Normalize UI Components to Design System
content.value_statement: Eliminates design inconsistencies by automatically detecting and fixing deviations from your design system. Ensures every component uses correct tokens, patterns, and established conventions.
actual_capabilities: Discovers and analyzes design system documentation, guidelines, and component libraries
Identifies deviations from design system patterns in typography, color, spacing, and layout
Replaces hard-coded values with design tokens and system components
Normalizes responsive behavior, accessibility patterns, and interaction states
Consolidates reusable components and removes orphaned code after refactoring
Ensures progressive disclosure and information hierarchy match established patterns
limitations: Requires existing design system documentation or style guide to reference
Cannot create new design patterns - only enforces existing ones
Read-only skill that analyzes code but requires user approval for changes
Depends on well-documented design tokens and component conventions

use_cases.title: Legacy UI Modernization
use_cases.description: Development teams migrating inherited codebases to design system standards can use this skill to systematically replace custom implementations with system components.

use_cases.title: Design System Enforcement
use_cases.description: UI/UX leads can ensure all new features follow established design patterns by running this skill during code review or before merging PRs.

use_cases.title: Component Library Standardization
use_cases.description: Teams building component libraries can use this skill to identify inconsistencies across components and ensure uniform API design and visual consistency.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Normalization
prompt_templates.prompt: Normalize the checkout page to match our design system standards.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick normalization of a specific page or feature

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the entire user dashboard section and create a detailed normalization plan. List all deviations from the design system before making changes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Thorough analysis requiring approval before implementation

prompt_templates.title: Pattern-Specific Fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Normalize the typography and spacing in the settings modal. Use design tokens instead of hard-coded values.
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeted fixes for specific design dimensions

prompt_templates.title: Post-Refactoring Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Normalize the new profile feature, then consolidate any reusable components you create into the shared UI library.
prompt_templates.scenario: Normalization with component extraction and sharing

output_examples.output: Analyzed navigation bar and found 12 deviations from design system: replaced custom spacing with spacing tokens (4px -> space-2, 16px -> space-4), updated font weights to match typography scale (600 -> semibold), swapped custom button implementation for design system Button component, aligned hover states with interaction patterns, and ensured ARIA labels match accessibility guidelines.

output_examples.input: Review the settings page for design consistency
output_examples.output: Settings page audit complete: found 8 inconsistencies including hard-coded colors (#3B82F6 should use primary-blue token), inconsistent padding (24px mixed with space-6), custom toggle switch (should use Toggle component), and misaligned responsive breakpoint (md instead of lg). Created normalization plan - awaiting approval to proceed.

best_practices: Always run frontend-design skill first to ensure design system context exists before normalization
Review the normalization plan before execution to understand all proposed changes
Test features thoroughly after normalization to ensure no UX regressions were introduced
Consolidate new reusable components into shared component paths instead of duplicating them
anti_patterns: Running normalization without existing design system documentation or style guides
Bypassing the planning phase and making changes without understanding context
Creating new one-off components instead of using existing design system equivalents
Sacrificing accessibility or usability for visual consistency alone

faq.question: What design systems does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill works with any design system that has documentation, including Material UI, Tailwind-based systems, custom component libraries, or style guides. It discovers and analyzes your specific design system automatically.

faq.question: Will this skill modify my code automatically?
faq.answer: No, this skill analyzes and creates a normalization plan first. You review the plan before any changes are made. You remain in control of all modifications.

faq.question: What if my project lacks a design system?
faq.answer: This skill requires existing design system documentation to work effectively. If no design system exists, use the teach-impeccable skill first to establish design principles and patterns.

faq.question: Can I normalize just part of a feature?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify a particular page, component, or design dimension like 'normalize the typography in the header' for targeted fixes rather than full feature normalization.

faq.question: Does this skill handle responsive design?
faq.answer: Yes, normalization includes responsive behavior alignment - ensuring breakpoints, mobile patterns, and layout systems match your design system standards.

faq.question: What happens to custom components during normalization?
faq.answer: The skill identifies when custom implementations should use design system components instead. After normalization, it helps consolidate reusable components into shared paths for future use.
